motions.title: Approve Public Works Committee Recommendations including Water & Sewer Bylaw Exemptions
motions.summary: Council approved recommendations from the Public Works Committee, which included approving exemptions to waterworks and sewer & drain bylaws, potentially impacting property development and related services across the city.
motions.full_text: That PWC 25-013, being the minutes of the Public Works Committee meeting held on Monday, November 10, 2025 (Item 7.3), be received and the recommendations contained therein be approved; and That the following correspondence item, be received: Correspondence from Anthony Salemi, Planner, Policy and Government Relations, West End Home Builders' Association respecting Minute Item 9.3, Report PW25063, Waterworks and Sewer & Drains By-laws Exemptions (City Wide) (Item 7.3 (a)).

motions.title: Allocate $1.5M for City-led Climate Change Initiatives
motions.summary: The City Council approved the allocation of $1,514,305 from the Climate Change Reserve to fund seven city-led initiatives aimed at promoting environmental sustainability and mitigating climate change impacts across Hamilton.
motions.full_text: That Report PED25240, dated November 12, 2025, respecting 2025 City-Led Projects Recommended for Climate Change Reserve Funding (City Wide) and the accompanying presentation, be received and the following recommendations be approved: (a) That the recommendation to allocate funds in the total amount of $1,514,305 million dollars in 2025 from the City's Climate Change Reserve (Account No 108062) in order to support seven City-led climate action initiatives, BE APPROVED; (b) That the update regarding Planning and Economic Development General Manager approved 2025 Climate Change Reserve funding allocations for Office of Climate Change Initiatives priority efforts BE RECEIVED; and, (c) That the update regarding progress with implementation of City-led and community-led initiatives that received support from the City's Climate Change Reserve in 2023 and 2024 BE RECEIVED.
